study_guide_title: GST 112 - Nigeria's People, Culture, and Values - Comprehensive Q&A on Citizenship, Leadership & National Development

questions.question: Who coined the name 'Nigeria'?
questions.explanation: Flora Shaw, a British journalist and the future wife of Lord Lugard, coined the name 'Nigeria' in an 1897 article.

questions.question: Nigeria became a republic in what year?
questions.explanation: Nigeria became a republic on October 1, 1963, with Nnamdi Azikiwe as the first President.

questions.question: Nigeria's national motto is:

options.b: Unity and Faith, Peace and Progress

questions.explanation: Nigeria's motto is 'Unity and Faith, Peace and Progress'.

questions.question: The amalgamation of Northern and Southern Nigeria took place in:
questions.explanation: The amalgamation of the Northern and Southern Protectorates of Nigeria took place in 1914 under Lord Lugard.

questions.question: Who was the first President of Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Nnamdi Azikiwe became the first President of Nigeria when it became a republic in 1963.

questions.question: What is the capital of Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Abuja became the capital of Nigeria on December 12, 1991, replacing Lagos.

questions.question: Nigeria gained independence on:
questions.explanation: Nigeria gained independence from Britain on October 1, 1960.

questions.question: Nigeria is often referred to as:
questions.explanation: Nigeria is often called the 'Giant of Africa' because of its large population and economy.

questions.question: How many geopolitical zones does Nigeria have?
questions.explanation: Nigeria has 6 geopolitical zones: North-West, North-East, North-Central, South-West, South-East, and South-South.

questions.question: How many states does Nigeria have?
questions.explanation: Nigeria has 36 states and the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Abuja.

questions.question: Nigeria's flag has how many colours?
questions.explanation: Nigeria's flag has two colours: green and white.

questions.question: Who designed the Nigerian flag?
questions.explanation: Pa Michael Taiwo Akinkunmi designed the Nigerian flag in 1959.

questions.question: Nigeria is located in which continent?
questions.explanation: Nigeria is located in West Africa.

questions.question: What was the first capital city of Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Lagos was the first capital of Nigeria before the capital was moved to Abuja in 1991.

questions.question: The first political party in Nigeria was:
questions.explanation: The Nigerian National Democratic Party (NNDP) was the first political party in Nigeria, founded by Herbert Macaulay in 1923.

questions.question: The Nigerian flag has the following colours:
questions.explanation: The Nigerian flag has vertical bands of green, white, and green.

questions.question: The current Nigerian national anthem was adopted in:
questions.explanation: The current national anthem 'Arise, O Compatriots' was adopted in 1978.

questions.question: The original Nigerian national anthem was composed by:
questions.explanation: Lillian Jean Williams wrote the lyrics of the original Nigerian national anthem 'Nigeria, We Hail Thee'.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a geopolitical zone?
questions.explanation: There is no 'North-Middle' zone. The 6 zones are North-West, North-East, North-Central, South-West, South-East, and South-South.

questions.question: Nigeria is a multicultural state because of its:
questions.explanation: Nigeria is multicultural due to its over 250 ethnic groups with diverse languages and cultures.

questions.question: Nigeria operates which system of government?
questions.explanation: Nigeria operates a Presidential system of government with an executive president.

questions.question: The Nigerian Coat of Arms features:
questions.explanation: The Nigerian Coat of Arms features an eagle and a wreath.

questions.question: Who was the first Prime Minister of Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Sir Abubakar Tafawa Balewa was the first and only Prime Minister of Nigeria (1960-1966).

questions.question: Nigeria's current national anthem 'Arise, O Compatriots' was composed by:
questions.explanation: Benedict Odiase composed the current Nigerian national anthem 'Arise, O Compatriots'.

questions.question: The Nigerian national currency is:
questions.explanation: The Nigerian national currency is the Naira (₦).

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a major ethnic group in Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Zulu is a South African ethnic group, not Nigerian. The three major ethnic groups in Nigeria are Hausa, Igbo, and Yoruba.

questions.question: The largest ethnic group in Northern Nigeria is:
questions.explanation: The Hausa/Fulani is the largest ethnic group in Northern Nigeria.

questions.question: The Yoruba ethnic group is predominantly found in:
questions.explanation: The Yoruba ethnic group is predominantly found in the South-Western part of Nigeria.

questions.question: The Igbo ethnic group is predominantly found in:
questions.explanation: The Igbo ethnic group is predominantly found in the South-Eastern part of Nigeria.

questions.question: How many ethnic groups does Nigeria have?
questions.explanation: Nigeria has over 250 distinct ethnic groups.

questions.question: The Argungu Fishing Festival is associated with which ethnic group?
questions.explanation: The Argungu Fishing Festival is a traditional festival of the Hausa/Fulani people in Kebbi State.

questions.question: Nigeria's ethnic diversity is a source of:
questions.explanation: Nigeria's ethnic diversity is a source of cultural richness, tourism, and unity.

questions.question: Ethnocentrism is:

options.a: Believing one's culture is superior to others

questions.explanation: Ethnocentrism is the belief that one's own culture is superior to others.

questions.question: Ethnic diversity in Nigeria can promote:
questions.explanation: Ethnic diversity can promote national unity through mutual respect, cultural tourism, and economic growth.

questions.question: Culture is defined as:

options.a: The total way of life of a people
options.b: Only the language of a people
options.c: Only the food of a people
options.d: Only the religion of a people

questions.explanation: Culture is the total way of life of a people, including their language, food, dressing, religion, and customs.

questions.question: Indigenous languages in Nigeria are important for:
questions.explanation: Indigenous languages play a key role in preserving cultural identity, communication, and education.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of Nigeria's cultural heritage?
questions.explanation: Nigeria's cultural heritage includes festivals, art and craft, traditional dances, and other customs.

questions.question: The three major ethnic groups in Nigeria are:
questions.explanation: The three major ethnic groups in Nigeria are Hausa, Igbo, and Yoruba.

questions.question: The diversity of Nigeria comes from its:
questions.explanation: Nigeria's diversity comes from its many ethnic groups, each with its own language and culture.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of culture?
questions.explanation: Culture preserves heritage, promotes unity, and provides identity to a people.

questions.question: The Yoruba ethnic group is mainly located in which region?
questions.explanation: The Yoruba are mainly located in the South-Western region of Nigeria.

questions.question: Which ethnic group is predominantly found in South-East Nigeria?
questions.explanation: The Igbo ethnic group is predominantly found in South-East Nigeria.

questions.question: One of the major challenges facing indigenous languages in Nigeria is:
questions.explanation: Indigenous languages face challenges from globalization, declining number of speakers, and government neglect.

questions.question: Nigeria is a multilingual country because:
questions.explanation: Nigeria is multilingual because it has over 500 indigenous languages spoken across its ethnic groups.

questions.question: The official language of Nigeria is:
questions.explanation: English is the official language of Nigeria, inherited from British colonial rule.

questions.question: Indigenous languages are important for:
questions.explanation: Indigenous languages are vital for preserving cultural identity and heritage.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a major function of indigenous languages in Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Promoting international diplomacy is not a major function of indigenous languages; English serves that purpose.

questions.question: The diversity of Nigeria is based on:
questions.explanation: Nigeria's diversity is based on its many ethnic groups, languages, and religions.

questions.question: Nigeria's cultural diversity is a source of:
questions.explanation: Cultural diversity is a source of strength and unity when managed well.

questions.question: The promotion of indigenous languages helps to:
questions.explanation: The promotion of indigenous languages helps to preserve cultural heritage.

questions.question: Citizenship by birth refers to:
questions.explanation: Citizenship by birth means being born to Nigerian parents, regardless of place of birth.

questions.question: Citizenship can be acquired through:
questions.explanation: Citizenship can be acquired through birth, registration, or naturalisation.

questions.question: How many types of citizenship are recognised in the Nigerian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Nigerian Constitution recognises three types of citizenship: by birth, by registration, and by naturalisation.

questions.question: Citizenship by naturalisation is granted by:
questions.explanation: Citizenship by naturalisation is granted by the President of Nigeria.

questions.question: Citizenship by registration under the Nigerian Constitution applies to:

options.c: Only those born to Nigerian parents

questions.explanation: Citizenship by registration applies to aliens (non-Nigerians) who meet certain conditions specified in the Constitution.

questions.question: A symbol of Nigeria's national identity is:
questions.explanation: The flag, Coat of Arms, and national anthem are all symbols of Nigeria's national identity.

questions.question: The national identity of Nigeria is shaped by:
questions.explanation: Nigeria's national identity is shaped by its diverse cultures, common values, and shared historical experiences.

questions.question: The Nigerian constitution is the:
questions.explanation: The Nigerian Constitution is the supreme law of the land, and any other law inconsistent with it is void.

questions.question: The three tiers of government in Nigeria are:
questions.explanation: Nigeria operates a three-tier system: Federal, State, and Local Government.

questions.question: The legislature in Nigeria is responsible for:
questions.explanation: The legislature (National Assembly) is responsible for making laws.

questions.question: The executive arm of government is responsible for:
questions.explanation: The executive branch implements and enforces laws.

questions.question: The judiciary is responsible for:
questions.explanation: The judiciary interprets laws and administers justice.

questions.question: The Nigerian Constitution recognises the following types of citizenship:
questions.explanation: The Constitution recognises three types of citizenship: by birth, by registration, and by naturalisation.

questions.question: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) is located in:
questions.explanation: The FCT is located in Abuja.

questions.question: A responsible citizen should:
questions.explanation: A responsible citizen obeys the law, pays taxes, and participates in civic activities.

questions.question: Civic education is important because it:
questions.explanation: Civic education promotes nationalism and responsible citizenship.

questions.question: The Constitution is important because it:
questions.explanation: The Constitution provides the governance framework, protects rights, and defines government structure.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of democracy?
questions.explanation: Respect for human rights is a key feature of democracy.

questions.question: The three arms of government are:
questions.explanation: The three arms of government are the Legislature, Executive, and Judiciary.

questions.question: Good governance is characterised by:
questions.explanation: Good governance is characterised by accountability, transparency, and the rule of law.

questions.question: Which of the following is a civic responsibility?
questions.explanation: Civic responsibilities include obeying the law, paying taxes, and voting in elections.

questions.question: The rule of law means:

options.a: Everyone is equal before the law
options.b: The President is above the law
options.c: Only citizens must obey the law

questions.explanation: Rule of law means that every person, regardless of status, is equal before the law.

questions.question: Effective citizenship includes:
questions.explanation: Effective citizenship involves active participation in community development.

questions.question: National identity is important because it:
questions.explanation: National identity fosters a sense of unity and belonging among citizens.

questions.question: The Nigerian Constitution was enacted to:

options.a: Provide the legal framework for governance

questions.explanation: The Constitution provides the legal framework, protects rights, and defines government structure.

questions.question: Patriotism is defined as:

options.a: Loyalty and love for one's country

questions.explanation: Patriotism is the love, loyalty, and devotion to one's country.

questions.question: Which of the following is a moral value?
questions.explanation: Honesty is a moral value; corruption, selfishness, and dishonesty are not.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a moral value?
questions.explanation: Corruption is not a moral value. Moral values include integrity, discipline, and honesty.

questions.question: Respect for elders is an example of:
questions.explanation: Respect for elders is a moral value in Nigerian society.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a moral value?
questions.explanation: Corruption is a vice, not a moral value.

questions.question: Moral values help to:
questions.explanation: Moral values help individuals distinguish between right and wrong.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an aesthetic value?
questions.explanation: Democracy is a political value, not an aesthetic value. Aesthetic values relate to beauty, art, and music.

questions.question: Which of the following is a moral virtue?
questions.explanation: Integrity is a moral virtue; greed, dishonesty, and selfishness are vices.

questions.question: One of the benefits of moral values is:
questions.explanation: Moral values promote peaceful coexistence in society.

questions.question: Moral values promote the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Moral values promote peaceful coexistence, social harmony, and national unity, not disunity.

questions.question: Integrity is a combination of:
questions.explanation: Integrity means being honest and having strong moral principles.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a challenge to nation building?
questions.explanation: Patriotism promotes nation building; corruption, ethnic rivalry, and tribalism are challenges.

questions.question: Which of the following is a national value?
questions.explanation: Discipline is a national value; indiscipline, corruption, and selfishness are not.

questions.question: An example of honesty is:

options.a: Returning lost property to the owner

questions.explanation: Returning lost property to the owner is an example of honesty.

questions.question: Which of the following is a moral value?
questions.explanation: Respect is a moral value; cheating, corruption, and indiscipline are not.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a moral value?
questions.explanation: Fraud is a vice, not a moral value.

questions.question: One of the most important national values is:
questions.explanation: Integrity is one of the most important national values.

questions.question: Moral values are important because they:
questions.explanation: Moral values guide human behaviour and promote good conduct.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a person of integrity?

options.a: Someone who is honest and upright

questions.explanation: A person of integrity is honest and upright in all their dealings.

questions.question: Patriotism is demonstrated by:
questions.explanation: Patriotism is demonstrated through paying taxes, obeying the law, and defending the nation.

questions.question: Which of the following values promotes national development?
questions.explanation: Hard work, discipline, and honesty all promote national development.

questions.question: Accountability means:
questions.explanation: Accountability means taking responsibility for one's actions and decisions.

questions.question: The importance of patriotism includes:
questions.explanation: Patriotism fosters national unity and development.

questions.question: Which of the following challenges to nation building?
questions.explanation: Ethnic rivalry, corruption, and tribalism all pose challenges to nation building.

questions.question: Moral values guide individuals to:
questions.explanation: Moral values help individuals distinguish between right and wrong.

questions.question: The Action Group (AG) was led by:
questions.explanation: The Action Group (AG) was led by Chief Obafemi Awolowo.

questions.question: The Nigerian National Democratic Party (NNDP) was founded by:
questions.explanation: The NNDP was founded by Herbert Macaulay in 1923.

questions.question: The National Council of Nigeria and the Cameroons (NCNC) was associated with:
questions.explanation: The NCNC was closely associated with Dr. Nnamdi Azikiwe.

questions.question: The Action Group (AG) was strongest in which region?
questions.explanation: The Action Group was strongest in the Western Region of Nigeria.

questions.question: Which of the following is a quality of a good leader?
questions.explanation: Integrity is a key quality of a good leader.

questions.question: The primary function of political parties is to:
questions.explanation: The primary function of political parties is to present candidates for elections.

questions.question: Political parties exist to:
questions.explanation: Political parties exist primarily to contest elections and form governments.

questions.question: The first political party in Nigeria was:
questions.explanation: The Nigerian National Democratic Party (NNDP) was the first political party in Nigeria, founded in 1923.

questions.question: Herbert Macaulay founded which political party?
questions.explanation: Herbert Macaulay founded the Nigerian National Democratic Party (NNDP).

questions.question: Obafemi Awolowo led which political party?
questions.explanation: Obafemi Awolowo led the Action Group (AG).

questions.question: The Northern People's Congress (NPC) was dominant in:
questions.explanation: The NPC was dominant in Northern Nigeria.

questions.question: The National Council of Nigeria and the Cameroons (NCNC) was associated with:
questions.explanation: The NCNC was closely associated with Dr. Nnamdi Azikiwe.

questions.question: The primary objective of a political party is to:
questions.explanation: The primary objective of a political party is to form a government.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a good leader?
questions.explanation: Integrity is a key characteristic of a good leader.

questions.question: Obafemi Awolowo was associated with which political party?
questions.explanation: Obafemi Awolowo founded and led the Action Group.

questions.question: Leadership is defined as:

options.a: The ability to influence others positively

questions.explanation: Leadership is the ability to influence and guide others towards achieving common goals.

questions.question: The primary role of political parties in a democracy is to:
questions.explanation: Political parties contest elections to form the government.

questions.question: Political parties educate the populace on:
questions.explanation: Political parties educate the public on political issues and governance.

questions.question: A good leader should possess:
questions.explanation: A good leader should have vision and integrity.

questions.question: Which of the following is a challenge to good leadership in Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Corruption is a major challenge to good leadership in Nigeria.

questions.question: A leader who serves with integrity will:
questions.explanation: A leader with integrity promotes national development.

questions.question: Responsible leadership involves:
questions.explanation: Responsible leadership means serving the people with dedication and selflessness.

questions.question: Which of the following undermines good political leadership?
questions.explanation: Electoral fraud undermines good political leadership.

questions.question: A political party is an organisation that aims to:
questions.explanation: A political party is an organised group that aims to control government through elections.

questions.question: The main function of a political party is to:
questions.explanation: Political parties nominate candidates to contest elections.

questions.question: Democracy is strengthened by:
questions.explanation: Democracy is strengthened by free, fair, and credible elections.

questions.question: Good governance is based on:
questions.explanation: Good governance is founded on the principles of accountability and transparency.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of democracy?
questions.explanation: Free and fair elections are a defining feature of democracy.

questions.question: The principle of good governance includes:
questions.explanation: Transparency is a key principle of good governance.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a principle of good governance?
questions.explanation: Nepotism is not a principle of good governance; it is a vice.

questions.question: Good governance is characterised by:

options.a: Accountability, transparency, and rule of law

questions.explanation: Good governance involves accountability, transparency, and the rule of law.

questions.question: A democratic government is formed through:
questions.explanation: A democratic government is formed through periodic elections.

questions.question: A military government comes to power through:
questions.explanation: A military government seizes power through a coup d'état.

questions.question: Democracy encourages:
questions.explanation: Democracy encourages citizen participation in the governance process.

questions.question: Which of the following can weaken democracy?
questions.explanation: Electoral violence weakens democracy by undermining free and fair elections.

questions.question: Self-reliance is important for:
questions.explanation: Self-reliance reduces dependence on foreign aid and promotes local development.

questions.question: Which of the following promotes economic self-reliance?
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurship promotes economic self-reliance by creating local businesses.

questions.question: Self-reliance encourages:
questions.explanation: Self-reliance encourages local production and reduces import dependence.

questions.question: A self-reliant nation depends on:
questions.explanation: A self-reliant nation uses its own resources and manpower to meet its needs.

questions.question: One of the benefits of self-reliance is:
questions.explanation: Self-reliance leads to economic independence.

questions.question: Democracy is a system of government in which:

options.a: The people have the right to vote

questions.explanation: Democracy is government by the people through their elected representatives.

questions.question: One of the functions of government is to:
questions.explanation: A primary function of government is to maintain law and order.

questions.question: The constitution of a country serves as:

options.a: The supreme law of the land

questions.explanation: The constitution is the supreme law that governs a country.

questions.question: Responsible leadership is characterised by:

options.a: Serving the people with dedication and integrity

questions.explanation: Responsible leadership means serving people with dedication and integrity.

questions.question: Nigeria operates which type of democracy?
questions.explanation: Nigeria operates a presidential system of democracy.

questions.question: The legislature is responsible for:
questions.explanation: The legislature makes laws for the country.

questions.question: The executive branch of government is responsible for:
questions.explanation: The executive implements and executes the laws of the land.

questions.question: The judiciary is responsible for:
questions.explanation: The judiciary interprets the laws and administers justice.

questions.question: Which of the following is essential for democracy to thrive?

options.a: Respect for the rule of law

questions.explanation: Respect for the rule of law is essential for democracy to thrive.

questions.question: A feature of democracy is:
questions.explanation: Regular, free, and fair elections are a feature of democracy.

questions.question: Development is defined as:
questions.explanation: Development refers to the gradual growth, progress, and improvement in various aspects of society.

questions.question: Which of the following hinders development?
questions.explanation: Corruption is a major hindrance to development.

questions.question: A self-reliant economy depends on:
questions.explanation: A self-reliant economy depends on its own local resources.

questions.question: Self-reliance means:
questions.explanation: Self-reliance means depending on one's own capabilities and resources.

questions.question: Import Substitution Industrialisation (ISI) aims to:

options.a: Replace imported goods with locally made ones

questions.explanation: ISI is a strategy that encourages local production to replace imported goods.

questions.question: Export-Oriented Industrialisation (EOI) focuses on:

options.a: Increasing exports of locally made goods

questions.explanation: EOI focuses on producing goods for export to earn foreign exchange.

questions.question: Development involves:

options.a: Improvement in the quality of life

questions.explanation: Development involves improving the quality of life of citizens.

questions.question: The main occupation of Nigerians before colonial rule was:
questions.explanation: Farming was the main occupation of Nigerians before colonial rule.

questions.question: For a nation to be self-reliant, it must:

options.a: Depend on its local resources and manpower

questions.explanation: Self-reliance requires using local resources and manpower.

questions.question: One of the ways to promote national development is through:
questions.explanation: Education and skill acquisition promote national development.

questions.question: Development is a process of:
questions.explanation: Development involves positive change and growth in society.

questions.question: Which of the following is a strategy for economic development?
questions.explanation: Import Substitution Industrialisation (ISI) is a strategy for economic development.

questions.question: Self-reliance helps in:
questions.explanation: Self-reliance reduces unemployment through local production.

questions.question: Which of the following promotes national development?
questions.explanation: Good governance promotes national development.

questions.question: ISI stands for:
questions.explanation: ISI stands for Import Substitution Industrialisation.

questions.question: EOI stands for:
questions.explanation: EOI stands for Export-Oriented Industrialisation.

questions.question: One of the objectives of self-reliance is to:
questions.explanation: Self-reliance aims to reduce dependence on foreign nations.

questions.question: Which of the following is an effect of corruption on national development?
questions.explanation: Corruption slows economic growth and hinders national development.

questions.question: One of the benefits of national development is:
questions.explanation: National development improves the standard of living of citizens.

questions.question: Development that meets present needs without compromising future generations is called:
questions.explanation: Sustainable development meets present needs while preserving resources for future generations.

questions.question: Which of the following sectors is critical for national development?
questions.explanation: Education is a critical sector for national development.

questions.question: Economic development focuses on:

options.a: Improving the economy of a nation

questions.explanation: Economic development focuses on improving the economic well-being of a nation.

questions.question: One of the causes of underdevelopment in Nigeria is:
questions.explanation: Corruption is a major cause of underdevelopment in Nigeria.

questions.question: The concept of self-reliance encourages:
questions.explanation: Self-reliance encourages entrepreneurship and innovation.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a challenge to national development?
questions.explanation: Patriotism is not a challenge; it promotes national development.

questions.question: The War Against Indiscipline (WAI) was introduced by:
questions.explanation: The War Against Indiscipline (WAI) was introduced by the military government of Muhammadu Buhari in 1984.

questions.question: Re-orientation means:

options.a: Changing people's attitudes and values positively

questions.explanation: Re-orientation involves changing attitudes and values in a positive direction.

questions.question: Operation Feed the Nation (OFN) was introduced to:
questions.explanation: OFN was a programme introduced to encourage food production and self-sufficiency.

questions.question: The National Orientation Agency (NOA) promotes:
questions.explanation: NOA promotes positive values, patriotism, and national orientation.

questions.question: WAI was designed to promote:
questions.explanation: WAI was designed to promote discipline, orderliness, and positive values.

questions.question: NOA is responsible for:
questions.explanation: NOA is responsible for promoting patriotism and national orientation.

questions.question: MAMSER was introduced by which government?
questions.explanation: MAMSER (Mass Mobilisation for Self-Reliance, Social Justice and Economic Recovery) was introduced by the Babangida administration.

questions.question: MAMSER stands for:

options.a: Mass Mobilisation for Self-Reliance, Social Justice and Economic Recovery
options.b: Mass Movement for Social and Economic Reforms
options.c: Ministry of Social and Economic Rehabilitation
options.d: Mobilisation for Social and Economic Rights

questions.explanation: MAMSER stands for Mass Mobilisation for Self-Reliance, Social Justice and Economic Recovery.

questions.question: MAMSER was replaced by:
questions.explanation: MAMSER was replaced by the National Orientation Agency (NOA).

questions.question: NOA is a government agency responsible for:
questions.explanation: NOA handles national orientation and re-orientation of citizens.

questions.question: WAI was an acronym for:
questions.explanation: WAI stands for War Against Indiscipline.

questions.question: The NYSC scheme was introduced to:
questions.explanation: The NYSC (National Youth Service Corps) was introduced to promote national unity and integration.

questions.question: National integration is best promoted by:
questions.explanation: Unity in diversity promotes national integration.

questions.question: One of the objectives of NOA is:
questions.explanation: NOA encourages positive values, patriotism, and good citizenship.

questions.question: NOA replaced which government programme?
questions.explanation: NOA replaced the MAMSER programme.

questions.question: Promoting good citizenship involves:
questions.explanation: Good citizenship involves obeying the constitution and fulfilling civic responsibilities.

questions.question: Which of the following promotes national integration?
questions.explanation: Inter-ethnic marriages promote national integration and unity.

questions.question: The National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) was established in:
questions.explanation: The NYSC was established in 1973 under the Gowon administration.

questions.question: Which of the following promotes peaceful coexistence in Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Mutual respect among different groups promotes peaceful coexistence.

questions.question: One of the ways to promote national integration is through:
questions.explanation: Tolerance and respect for diversity promote national integration.

questions.question: Social engineering refers to:
questions.explanation: Social engineering involves changing societal attitudes and behaviours in a positive direction.

questions.question: Which of the following is an effect of corruption?
questions.explanation: Corruption leads to underdevelopment.

questions.question: National integration can be strengthened through:
questions.explanation: Equal opportunities for all citizens strengthen national integration.

questions.question: Which of the following is a challenge to national unity?
questions.explanation: Ethnic rivalry is a challenge to national unity.

questions.question: Which of the following promotes peace?
questions.explanation: Religious tolerance promotes peace among diverse groups.
